turns.user_message: I’ve finally accepted that I’m just not a very interesting person. It never really used to bother me when I realized I was just blending into the background, but lately it feels heavier. I catch myself listening to conversations and realizing I have absolutely nothing valuable to add. I just sit there nodding along while everyone else shares these vibrant stories. It’s strange how something so ordinary can suddenly feel like a glaring flaw.

I keep noticing how I naturally shrink away whenever I’m in a group setting. What used to be a quiet preference has turned into this exhausting habit of making myself invisible. I rehearse simple thoughts in my head for hours, only to delete them entirely before speaking out loud. Every time I try to join in, my words come out flat and completely unconvincing. I feel like I’m constantly apologizing for taking up space without even saying a word.

I’m completely convinced that my presence is just a burden to anyone who has to deal with me. The quiet acceptance I used to carry around has completely shattered into this heavy, suffocating dread. I spend my entire day analyzing every single interaction, hunting for proof that I’m fundamentally unremarkable and deeply forgettable. It’s exhausting to carry this constant weight of knowing I’ll never truly matter to anyone. I just want to disappear because I know the world would honestly be better off without my dull energy.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
